message:
Add panic-cleanup suppression for catch_unwind sites and improve doctor suite error reporting

Two independent improvements:

1. Panic-cleanup suppression (ftui-core, ftui-demo-showcase, ftui-runtime):
   The terminal session module installs a panic hook that performs
   best-effort terminal cleanup (restoring cooked mode, disabling
   alternate screen, etc.). However, when catch_unwind is used
   intentionally — as in visual effects rendering and reactive batch
   flush — the panic hook would unnecessarily run terminal cleanup
   for panics that are expected to be caught and recovered.

   - terminal_session.rs: Added thread-local PANIC_CLEANUP_SUPPRESS_DEPTH
     counter, with_panic_cleanup_suppressed() RAII helper using a
     SuppressGuard that decrements on drop (handles nested scopes and
     unwinding correctly), and panic_cleanup_suppressed() query.
     The install_panic_hook() now checks this flag before calling
     best_effort_cleanup(). Added test verifying nested suppression
     scope semantics.
   - lib.rs: Re-exported with_panic_cleanup_suppressed from ftui-core,
     with a no-op fallback on wasm32/non-crossterm targets.
   - app.rs (ftui-demo-showcase): Wrapped the screen view dispatch
     catch_unwind in with_panic_cleanup_suppressed so that panicking
     screens do not trigger terminal restoration.
   - visual_effects.rs: Wrapped the effect renderer catch_unwind in
     with_panic_cleanup_suppressed for the same reason.
   - batch.rs (ftui-runtime): Wrapped the deferred-callback catch_unwind
     in with_panic_cleanup_suppressed to prevent terminal cleanup during
     reactive flush panics.

2. Doctor suite report failure handling (doctor_franktentui/suite.rs):
   Previously, if the report generation step failed, the error was
   logged to a file but the suite still reported success. Now:
   - A report_failed flag tracks report generation errors.
   - The UI shows a warning instead of success when reports fail.
   - The JSON output includes report_failed and sets status to "failed"
     when report generation fails.
   - The function returns a DoctorError with exit code 1 when reports
     fail, ensuring CI pipelines catch the failure.

3. vfx_harness.jsonl: Regenerated deterministic VFX frame hashes
   (updated hash values from visual effects rendering changes).

4. .beads/issues.jsonl: Updated bead metadata for the above work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1oyeg","title":"Parser: DA/DSR device queries + character set designation (G0/G1) + single shifts","description":"## Background
The VT parser handles 56 action variants covering most core terminal sequences, but lacks several important features needed for application compatibility.

## Gaps to Fill

### Device Attribute / Status Reports (Tier 1 — critical handshake)
- DA1 (CSI c / CSI 0 c): Primary device attributes — many CLI programs send this as a startup handshake
- DA2 (CSI > c / CSI > 0 c): Secondary device attributes (terminal type/version)
- DSR (CSI 5 n): Device status report — "are you OK?" query
- CPR (CSI 6 n): Cursor position report request

### Character Set Designation (Tier 1 — affects rendering)
- G0 designate (ESC ( B / ESC ( 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- G1 designate (ESC ) B / ESC ) 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- These are used by ncurses, vim, tmux for box-drawing characters

### Single Shift (Tier 2 — completeness)
- SS2 (ESC N): Single shift to G2
- SS3 (ESC O): Single shift to G3

## Deliverables
- New Action variants for DA1, DA2, DSR, CPR, DesignateCharset, SingleShift
- Parser dispatch for all new sequences
- Unit tests for each new sequence
- Conformance fixtures for key sequences
- Wire new actions in test harnesses (no-op in harness is fine)

## Testing
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:09:19.791783954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52.264271402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:47.890125157Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1048,"issue_id":"bd-1oyeg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: 8 new Action variants (DeviceAttributes, DeviceAttributesSecondary, DeviceStatusReport, CursorPositionReport, DesignateCharset, SingleShift2, SingleShift3), CSI dispatch for c/>c/n, ESC dispatch for (/)/*//+/N/O, EscCharset state, reply engine integration, 15 unit tests, 3 conformance fixtures. Changes landed in 500b69db.","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"Implement LRU width cache for text measurement","description":"# Implement LRU Width Cache for Text Measurement

## Background
Plan Section 1.4 and Appendix D.3 explicitly mention the "LRU width cache" from rich_rust as a key optimization. Text width measurement is a hot path (called for every render) and must be cached.

## Why LRU Cache?
- Unicode width calculation is expensive (grapheme segmentation + width lookup)
- Same text strings appear repeatedly (labels, status text, etc.)
- Cache hit rate expected to be high for typical UIs
- Prevents O(n) per-frame width recalculation

## Design
```rust
/// Width cache with LRU eviction
pub struct WidthCache {
    cache: LinkedHashMap<u64, usize>,  // hash -> width
    capacity: usize,
}

impl WidthCache {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LinkedHashMap::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
        }
    }

    /// Get cached width or compute and cache
    pub fn get_or_compute(&mut self, text: &str, compute: impl FnOnce() -> usize) -> usize {
        let hash = hash_text(text);
        
        if let Some(&width) = self.cache.get(&hash) {
            // Move to front (LRU refresh)
            self.cache.get_refresh(&hash);
            return width;
        }
        
        let width = compute();
        
        // Evict if over capacity
        while self.cache.len() >= self.capacity {
            self.cache.pop_front();
        }
        
        self.cache.insert(hash, width);
        width
    }
    
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}

// Use FNV or xxhash for fast hashing
fn hash_text(text: &str) -> u64 {
    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
    let mut hasher = FnvHasher::default();
    text.hash(&mut hasher);
    hasher.finish()
}
```

## Integration Points
- `RenderContext` holds a `RefCell<WidthCache>`
- `Text::cell_length()` uses the cache
- `Segment::cell_length()` uses the cache
- Cache is cleared on theme change (fonts can affect width)

## Cache Sizing
- Default: 1024 entries (covers typical UI with headroom)
- Configurable via `WidthCache::new(capacity)`
- Memory footprint: ~32 bytes per entry (hash + width + list pointers)

## Performance Requirements
- Cache hit: < 50ns (hash + lookup)
- Cache miss: < 5µs (grapheme segmentation + width calculation)
- Hash collision handling: rare, just recompute

## Tests Required
- [ ] Cache hits return correct cached value
- [ ] Cache misses compute and cache
- [ ] LRU eviction works correctly
- [ ] Clear() empties cache
- [ ] Thread safety if needed (RefCell for single-threaded)
- [ ] Performance benchmarks

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LRU cache implemented with configurable capacity
- [ ] Integrated into text measurement hot path
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show significant speedup
- [ ] No correctness regressions in width measurement

## Location
`ftui-text/src/cache.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:17:08.580451576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:57:35.495094915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:57:35.495076190Z","close_reason":"Implemented LRU width cache with FxHash, preload support, stats tracking, and optional thread-local cache. 28 new tests (117 total in crate).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p1kn","title":"Add edge-case tests for terminal_model.rs: CellDiff Display, cursor wrap at bottom, LF/BS/Tab boundaries, CUP f-variant, erase display ToStart, SGR truecolor insufficient params, multi-byte UTF-8, out-of-bounds access, Rgb/ModelCell/ModelStyle traits","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:54:45.207067480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:05:46.581598624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:05:46.581579418Z","close_reason":"Completed: 84 total terminal_model.rs tests (51 new edge-case tests covering CellDiff Display, cursor boundaries, SGR parsing, erase operations, OSC8 hyperlinks, out-of-bounds access, and trait coverage)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv","title":"doctor_franktentui: comprehensive no-mock test coverage + e2e observability hardening","description":"Scope: doctor_franktentui only. Build a complete and realistic verification stack centered on real behavior (no mocks/fake business logic). Deliver module-level unit coverage targets, integration tests for process + RPC flows, full e2e scripts, and high-fidelity logs for failure triage.

Definition of done:
- Unit + integration tests cover all production modules and critical branches.
- E2E scripts cover happy-path and failure-path flows.
- E2E logging is detailed enough to diagnose failures without rerunning locally.
- CI can run the suite and surface artifacts.
- Gaps and residual risk are explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:01.802633507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:17.568144406Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","e2e","quality","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1235,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution plan by dependency phase:
Phase A (spec): bd-1p2xv.1.
Phase B (test implementation): bd-1p2xv.2, bd-1p2xv.3, bd-1p2xv.4, bd-1p2xv.5.
Phase C (workflow-level verification): bd-1p2xv.7 then bd-1p2xv.6.
Phase D (quality gates + rollout): bd-1p2xv.8 then bd-1p2xv.9.

Non-negotiables:
- No fake business logic stubs for doctor_franktentui behavior.
- Every failing test/e2e case must emit enough structured evidence to debug offline.
- CI artifacts must preserve both human-readable and machine-readable outputs.","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:17Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.1","title":"doctor_franktentui test matrix + acceptance contract","description":"Create an explicit verification matrix for every production file in crates/doctor_franktentui/src.

Deliverables:
- Module-by-module matrix with critical behaviors and failure modes.
- Definition of what counts as no-mock realism for unit/integration/e2e levels.
- Required test cases per command: capture/suite/report/doctor/seed-demo.
- Required observability fields for logs and artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Matrix checked into repo docs.
- Every subsequent task in this epic references matrix rows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:19.204449671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:37.150751374Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:37.150732709Z","close_reason":"Completed: added crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md with no-mock contract, behavior matrix, and bead-to-row traceability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","planning","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:19.204449671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1236,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Suggested matrix schema columns:
- module
- behavior_id
- behavior_description
- level(unit/integration/e2e)
- deterministic_input
- expected_output_or_side_effect
- failure_signature
- log_artifacts_required
- status(planned/implemented/verified)

Required behavior groups:
- CLI parsing and precedence
- profile + arg merge rules
- command execution + exit code mapping
- RPC request/response + retries
- file artifact generation and report integrity
- json-mode output contract","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:17Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.2","title":"Unit tests: util/keyseq/profile/runmeta/tape edge-to-edge behavior","description":"Expand deterministic unit coverage for pure modules with real parsing/serialization behavior and edge conditions.

Target files:
- util.rs
- keyseq.rs
- profile.rs

- tape.rs

Coverage requirements:
- Happy paths, malformed input, boundary values, and normalization rules.
- Output format determinism checks (JSON/text ordering where applicable).
- Explicit tests for new output-mode behavior (JSON vs human output gating).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:19.254278988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:37.236923114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:19.254278988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:45.643361920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1237,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Granular subtask checklist:
1) util.rs: parse_duration_value, normalize_http_path, shell_single_quote, relative_to, output integration helpers.

3) profile.rs: malformed env lines, whitespace normalization, missing profiles.

+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.3","title":"Unit tests: capture/suite/doctor/seed argument and policy resolution","description":"Add granular unit tests for command argument merging, profile overlays, compatibility rules, and policy errors.

1) capture.rs: precedence matrix (app-command vs legacy-runtime flags), seed flags compatibility, snapshot-required semantics, timeout fallback status.
2) suite.rs: fail-fast/keep-going precedence, app-command omission in legacy mode, report-failure propagation.
3) doctor.rs: help-check suppression behavior, full vs dry smoke execution path selection.
4) seed.rs: output-mode branch behavior and timeout loop behavior.
5) Add table-driven tests for branch-heavy logic to keep diffs readable and deterministic.","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:18Z"},{"id":1246,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix row linkage: DF-CLI-001..002, DF-ERR-001..002, DF-CAP-001..004, DF-SUITE-001..002, DF-DOCTOR-001..005, DF-SEED-006 (see crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md).","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:27Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.4","title":"Integration tests: seed-demo RPC retries/timeouts against real local HTTP server","description":"Build integration tests that exercise seed-demo RPC flows with a real ephemeral local server (TcpListener + HTTP responses), not mocks.

Coverage requirements:
- transient failures recovered by retry logic.
- non-JSON and JSON-RPC error handling.
- timeout behavior in wait_for_server.
- auth header propagation and endpoint path normalization.

Artifacts:
- deterministic fixture transcripts for request/response exchanges.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:20.111580571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:27.596427601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","integration","rpc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:20.111580571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:46.470957617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1239,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Real-server integration checklist:
1) Start ephemeral local server with predictable scripted responses per request index.
2) Cases: initial connection refused then success, empty body, non-JSON body, JSON-RPC error body, success body.
3) Assert retry backoff count and stop conditions.
4) Assert Authorization header when auth token provided.
5) Assert normalized endpoint path handling (leading/trailing slash).
6) Persist transcript JSONL for each case under temp artifact root for debugging.","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:19Z"},{"id":1248,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix row linkage: DF-SEED-001..005 (see crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md).","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:27Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.5","title":"Integration tests: subprocess orchestration for doctor/capture/suite/report","description":"Add integration tests that invoke the real doctor_franktentui binary as a subprocess and assert filesystem artifacts + exit semantics.

Coverage requirements:
- doctor dry-run/full smoke behavior (including help-check behavior).
- capture dry-run and non-dry metadata writes.
- suite success/failure/report-failure propagation.
- report generation for multi-run suites.

Implementation constraints:
- Use temp directories and local process execution.
- No mock process layer.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.155643150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:27.485289786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","integration","process","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.155643150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:46.883960345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1240,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Subprocess integration checklist:
1) Spawn built doctor_franktentui binary with temp run roots and project fixture dirs.
2) Assert stdout/stderr contracts for json-mode and human mode.
3) Assert run_meta.json, run_summary.txt, suite_manifest.json, and report outputs are generated with expected fields.
4) Assert non-zero exits for intentional failure paths and verify message text includes cause.
5) Validate idempotent reruns in isolated directories.","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:19Z"},{"id":1247,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix row linkage: DF-DOCTOR-001..005, DF-CAP-005..009, DF-SUITE-003..005, DF-REPORT-004 (see crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md).","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:27Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.6","title":"E2E script: failure-path matrix for doctor_franktentui with rich diagnostics","description":"Add a second doctor_franktentui e2e script that exercises expected failure paths and confirms precise error semantics.

Failure matrix examples:
- invalid profile
- incompatible flags (seed-required without seed-demo)
- missing/invalid binary in legacy mode
- report generation failure surfaced by suite
- timeout and retry boundaries in seed path

Diagnostics requirements:
- per-case isolated log directories
- expected vs actual exit code assertions
- compact machine-readable summary JSON for CI consumption","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.260299860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:29.333896005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","e2e","logging","negative","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.260299860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:48.986762709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:49.398627846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:49.821533090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:50.227946150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1242,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Failure-matrix e2e script requirements:
- Each case isolated in its own work dir.
- Each case declares expected exit code + expected message regex.
- Capture and retain stderr/stdout even on expected failures.
- Produce machine-readable case_results.json with fields: case_id, expected_exit, actual_exit, pass, key_artifact_paths.
- Fail script if any case unexpectedly passes/fails or missing diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:20Z"},{"id":1251,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix row linkage: DF-E2E-002..003 (see crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md).","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1p2xv.7","title":"E2E script: doctor_franktentui happy-path workflow with forensic logging","description":"Create a dedicated e2e script under scripts/ for doctor_franktentui happy-path validation.

Workflow minimum:
- doctor command (environment + smoke checks)
- capture run with deterministic profile
- suite run across representative profiles
- report verification

Logging requirements:
- timestamped run root
- full stdout/stderr capture per step
- command line + env snapshot + tool version capture
- checksum/hash of run_meta and summary artifacts
- explicit pass/fail summary block","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.284983745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:28.475957538Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor_franktentui","e2e","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:35.284983745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:47.312171663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:47.737864662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:48.153621180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1p2xv.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:46:48.563230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1241,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Happy-path e2e script requirements:
- strict bash mode (set -euo pipefail)
- per-step timer and exit capture
- environment dump (selected vars only), tool versions, git rev, command manifest
- logs/{step}.stdout.log and logs/{step}.stderr.log
- artifact manifest JSON containing paths, checksums, file sizes, and timestamps
- final summary table + JSON output for CI parser","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:47:20Z"},{"id":1249,"issue_id":"bd-1p2xv.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix row linkage: DF-E2E-001 (see crates/doctor_franktentui/TEST_MATRIX.md).","created_at":"2026-02-17T22:50:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1plj","title":"Async Task Manager E2E: Anytime-Valid Flake Detector (e-process)","description":"## Goal
Detect flaky timing regressions in E2E tests without inflating false positives, using anytime-valid statistics.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Compute an e-value for latency/outcome deviations during E2E runs. For sub-Gaussian residuals r_t (mean 0 under null), use:
- e_t = exp(lambda * r_t - (lambda^2 * sigma^2) / 2)
- E_t = product_{i=1..t} e_i
Fail early only when E_t > 1/alpha, preserving validity under optional stopping.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Early fail only with strong evidence; no false alarms in stable runs.
- Works with variable-length test runs.
- Logs e-value trajectory and decision point.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_eprocess_threshold: failure at E_t > 1/alpha.
- unit_eprocess_nonnegative: e-values never negative.
- unit_optional_stopping: stopping early preserves decision validity (simulated).

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Inject synthetic latency spikes; verify early failure and log evidence.
- No spike: no failure; log shows stable e-values.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

Features: FlakeConfig, FlakeDetector, sub-Gaussian e-value formula, batch observation, variance estimation, evidence logging, 12 unit tests, E2E script. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1po2","title":"Implement Terminal Capability Probing tests","description":"# Implement Terminal Capability Probing tests

## Background
Part of Terminal Capability Auto-Upgrade (bd-3227). Tests ensure probing works safely and correctly.

## Unit Tests
- probe_sends_correct_escape_sequence: Each ProbeableCapability sends correct CSI/OSC
- timeout_detection_works: Pending probes time out correctly
- response_parsing_matches_terminal_behavior: Parse realistic terminal responses
- confirmed_capability_is_remembered: confirmed.contains() returns true after confirmation
- denied_capability_is_not_confirmed: Explicit denial handled correctly
- multiple_probes_tracked_independently: Multiple in-flight probes work

## Integration Tests
- real_terminal_probing_succeeds: Against known terminal emulators (iTerm2, WezTerm, etc)
- graceful_fallback_on_timeout: App continues with conservative caps on timeout
- capability_upgrade_improves_output: Confirmed truecolor enables better colors

## Property Tests
- probe_id_uniqueness: Probe IDs never collide within session
- timeout_detection_is_monotonic: Once timed out, stays timed out

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass in CI with known terminals
- [ ] No hangs or panics on malformed responses","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:12.381493093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.481207717Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.481141463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1po2","depends_on_id":"bd-3227","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
